meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: The Freelancer's Guide to Shipping Faster
content.seo_description: Overcome coding blocks with this expert AI debugging checklist. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to solve complex bugs and ship professional SaaS apps faster.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with cryptic stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for automated diagnostic analysis and Claude Code for iterative CLI-based refactoring, you can identify, isolate, and resolve complex application bugs in minutes. This workflow transforms debugging from a tedious bottleneck into a high-velocity, automated process that keeps your client projects on schedule.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your deadline is breathing down your neck, and you are staring at a recursive error that makes no sense. You’re stuck in the 'junior loop'—endless trial and error, frustration mounting, and zero progress. Your client expects a professional, high-performance SaaS product, not an excuse for why the build is broken.

It is time to stop debugging like an amateur and start engineering like an architect. By leveraging AI-native tools, you can offload the mental heavy lifting of pattern recognition and syntax correction. This isn't just about fixing a bug; it's about building a robust, repeatable system that ensures your code stays clean, functional, and ready for production, regardless of how complex the feature set becomes.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Domain
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the boundaries of the bug to prevent side effects in other modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided stack trace and local file structure to identify the exact module where the state corruption originates. Map the dependency chain.

steps.title: Synthesize Diagnostic Logs
steps.description: Feed your logs into the AI to find patterns that are invisible to the naked eye.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review these error logs and identify the recurring pattern in memory allocation before the crash occurs.

steps.title: Generate a Minimal Reproducible Case
steps.description: Create a sandbox environment to test the fix without risking the integrity of your main production codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal test script that replicates this specific bug in an isolated environment.

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Use AI to write the fix while maintaining your existing coding conventions and style guide.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Rewrite the function in this file to handle null pointer exceptions according to the established project coding style.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/core/logic.ts --prompt 'Apply the fix identified in the repro test'

steps.title: Verify Regression Safety
steps.description: Run a full suite of tests to ensure your fix didn't break existing functionality elsewhere.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of regression tests that ensure this bug does not recur during future refactors.

pro_tips: Always define your project's architectural constraints in the system prompt to prevent the AI from suggesting 'quick and dirty' code.
Use Claude Code's 'review' feature on every PR to catch edge cases you might have missed during the heat of debugging.
When debugging state management, ask Antigravity to visualize the state flow, which often reveals logic gaps in asynchronous calls.
Keep your context window clean by deleting temporary test files after the bug is resolved; AI performs better with less irrelevant code in memory.
Treat your AI agent as a junior developer: provide explicit step-by-step instructions rather than broad 'fix this' requests.
content.next_steps: After resolving your current bug, document the fix in a dedicated 'Knowledge Base' folder in your repo so you can reference it when similar issues arise in future client projects.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to turn your debugging process into a competitive advantage. Stop losing hours to frustration and start shipping high-quality code that delights your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
